slides.title: Your skincare *makes redness worse*.
slides.sub: Most facial redness isn't a product you're missing. It's heat, alcohol and a barrier you've stripped.

slides.title: Stop over-exfoliating
slides.body: Strong actives and daily scrubs *wreck your skin barrier*, and a raw barrier reads as redness. Do far less.

slides.title: Alcohol flushes the face
slides.body: Alcohol *dilates facial vessels*, and repeated flushing can settle into a persistent ruddy tone. Drink less.

slides.title: Cool the heat down
slides.body: Hot showers, saunas and spicy food *flush reactive skin*. Go lukewarm and notice your own triggers.

slides.title: Some redness starts in the gut
slides.body: For some people, redness tracks gut irritation. *More fiber, fewer triggers* may calm it. Minor and individual.

slides.title: Sleep lowers inflammation
slides.body: Poor sleep *raises inflammation* and leaves skin ruddier. Deep, regular sleep drops the baseline flush.

slides.title: Calmer, even, *less red*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food & water that lower the inflammation behind redness — in one daily score.

caption:
Plot twist: the redness might be your skincare. Stripped barriers, heat and alcohol flush the face more than they fix it. 🔥

Do less, cool your showers, drink less, sleep deep. Save this before your next routine. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food & water that calm inflammation → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Your Skincare Is Making Redness Worse
